channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Cape Verde's historic World Cup ride ends after pushing Argentina to the brink
item.description: Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.
item.content:encoded:
Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.

(Image credit: Lynne Sladky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran begins dayslong funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran began a dayslong funeral Saturday for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, months after an airstrike killed him at the start of the war. He was 86.
item.content:encoded:
Iran began a dayslong funeral Saturday for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, months after an airstrike killed him at the start of the war. He was 86.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Reflections on America's 250th birthday
item.description: NPR member station reporters across the U.S. asked people how they are thinking about their country on its semiquincentennial.
item.content:encoded:
NPR member station reporters across the U.S. asked people how they are thinking about their country on its semiquincentennial.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Susan Bence

item.title: After weeks of speculation, Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wed in New York
item.description: Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.
item.content:encoded:
Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.

(Image credit: Gregory Shamus)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: A family steps in to save the dying casino town of Primm, Nevada
item.description: Primm, Nev., a once-thriving casino town on the border with California, was on the verge of fading away for good. The family it was named for has stepped in and faces the challenge of reviving it.
item.content:encoded:
Primm, Nev., a once-thriving casino town on the border with California, was on the verge of fading away for good. The family it was named for has stepped in and faces the challenge of reviving it.

(Image credit: Krystal Ramirez for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Christopher Intagliata

item.title: Iran plans dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei after war death
item.description: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.
item.content:encoded:
The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Nearly half of Americans surveyed don't know what America 250 commemorates
item.description: A new national poll reveals a striking paradox in public sentiment ahead of America's 250th anniversary: a disconnect between Americans' strong patriotic pride and their lack of civic knowledge.
item.content:encoded:
A new national poll reveals a striking paradox in public sentiment ahead of America's 250th anniversary: a disconnect between Americans' strong patriotic pride and their lack of civic knowledge.

(Image credit: Juan Mabromata)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Just gave birth or are about to? Share your story and questions about costs
item.description: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.
item.content:encoded: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.

item.title: Former USAID head grieves its closure while hoping for its future
item.description: Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.
item.content:encoded:
Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
item.description: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.
item.content:encoded:
Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.

(Image credit: Hulton Archive)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek
